edges.description: A functionally incompetent primary cilium cannot correctly transduce cilium-dependent developmental signaling, principally Hedgehog and non-canonical Wnt / planar cell polarity.
Defective ciliary signaling during development disrupts cranial suture, skeletal, and ectodermal-appendage morphogenesis.
Ciliary dysfunction in renal tubular epithelium, hepatobiliary tissue, and photoreceptors produces the progressive multiorgan involvement of CED.
Disrupted cilium-dependent Hedgehog and PCP signaling in the developing cranium, growth plate, and ectodermal appendages produces the craniofacial, skeletal, and ectodermal phenotype.

nodes.description: Abnormal nail morphology, an ectodermal feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: ectodermal features (widely spaced hypoplastic teeth, hypodontia, sparse hair, skin laxity, abnormal nails)

nodes.description: Shortening of the digits, part of the skeletal involvement in CED.

evidence.snippet: skeletal involvement (narrow thorax, shortened proximal limbs, syndactyly, polydactyly, brachydactyly)

nodes.description: The primary cilium is the obligate organelle for vertebrate Hedgehog signal transduction and coordinates non-canonical Wnt / planar cell polarity (PCP) signaling. IFT-A-driven ciliary assembly and trafficking defects derange these pathways, perturbing the developmental signaling that patterns the skull, skeleton, and tubular organs.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular basis of ciliary defects caused by compound heterozygous IFT144/WDR19 mutations found in cranioectodermal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: When exogenously expressed in IFT144-knockout (KO) cells, IFT144(L710S) as well as IFT144(WT) rescued both moderately compromised ciliogenesis and the abnormal localization of ciliary proteins.

nodes.description: The developmental arm of CED. Impaired cilium-dependent signaling disturbs cranial suture patterning (producing sagittal craniosynostosis and dolichocephaly), endochondral skeletal growth (narrow thorax, short proximal limbs, brachydactyly, polydactyly), and the morphogenesis of ectodermal appendages (sparse hair, hypoplastic widely spaced teeth, abnormal nails). Dolichocephaly secondary to sagittal craniosynostosis is the manifestation that distinguishes CED from most other ciliopathies.

evidence.snippet: Cranioectodermal dysplasia (CED) is a ciliopathy with skeletal involvement (narrow thorax, shortened proximal limbs, syndactyly, polydactyly, brachydactyly), ectodermal features (widely spaced hypoplastic teeth, hypodontia, sparse hair, skin laxity, abnormal nails), joint laxity, growth deficiency, and characteristic facial features
Dolichocephaly, often secondary to sagittal craniosynostosis, is a primary manifestation that distinguishes CED from most other ciliopathies.

nodes.description: Skin laxity, an ectodermal/connective-tissue feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: ectodermal features (widely spaced hypoplastic teeth, hypodontia, sparse hair, skin laxity, abnormal nails)

nodes.description: Long, narrow head shape, typically secondary to sagittal craniosynostosis; a primary manifestation of CED.

evidence.snippet: Dolichocephaly, often secondary to sagittal craniosynostosis, is a primary manifestation that distinguishes CED from most other ciliopathies.

nodes.description: Prominent forehead, part of the characteristic CED facial gestalt.

evidence.snippet: characteristic facial features (frontal bossing, low-set simple ears, high forehead, telecanthus, epicanthal folds, full cheeks, everted lower lip)

nodes.description: Hepatic fibrosis as part of the hepatobiliary ciliopathy spectrum of CED.

evidence.snippet: Hepatic fibrosis and retinal dystrophy are also observed.

nodes.description: Congenital absence of teeth, an ectodermal feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: ectodermal features (widely spaced hypoplastic teeth, hypodontia, sparse hair, skin laxity, abnormal nails)

nodes.description: Biallelic mutations in IFT-A complex genes (IFT122, WDR35, IFT43, WDR19, and the additional CED genes IFT140 and IFT52) disrupt retrograde intraflagellar transport. IFT-A drives retrograde (tip-to-base) cargo trafficking along the ciliary axoneme; its loss impairs primary cilium assembly, maintenance, and the gated compartmentalization of signaling machinery, producing a structurally deficient or functionally incompetent cilium.

evidence.reference_title: Cranioectodermal Dysplasia, Sensenbrenner syndrome, is a ciliopathy caused by mutations in the IFT122 gene.
evidence.snippet: Because IFT122 plays an important role in the assembly and maintenance of eukaryotic cilia, we investigated patient fibroblasts and found significantly reduced frequency and length of primary cilia as compared to controls.

evidence.reference_title: Ciliopathies with skeletal anomalies and renal insufficiency due to mutations in the IFT-A gene WDR19.
evidence.snippet: WDR19 encodes IFT144, a member of the intraflagellar transport (IFT) complex A that drives retrograde ciliary transport.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular basis of ciliary defects caused by compound heterozygous IFT144/WDR19 mutations found in cranioectodermal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in genes encoding the IFT-A subunits (IFT43, IFT121/WDR35, IFT122, IFT139/TTC21B, IFT140 and IFT144/WDR19) often result in skeletal ciliopathies, including cranioectodermal dysplasia (CED).

nodes.description: Generalized joint laxity is a recognized core feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: joint laxity

nodes.description: Narrow thorax with short ribs contributing to respiratory compromise; part of the skeletal ciliopathy phenotype, which may require mechanical ventilation in newborns with pulmonary hypoplasia.

evidence.snippet: Cranioectodermal dysplasia (CED) is a ciliopathy with skeletal involvement (narrow thorax, shortened proximal limbs, syndactyly, polydactyly, brachydactyly)

nodes.description: Most affected children develop nephronophthisis, frequently progressing to end-stage kidney disease in infancy or childhood, the major cause of morbidity and mortality in CED.

evidence.snippet: Most affected children develop nephronophthisis that often leads to end-stage kidney disease in infancy or childhood, a major cause of morbidity and mortality.

nodes.description: Extra digits on the ulnar/fibular side of the limb, present in a subset of CED patients.

evidence.snippet: skeletal involvement (narrow thorax, shortened proximal limbs, syndactyly, polydactyly, brachydactyly)

nodes.description: The degenerative multiorgan arm of CED. Ciliary dysfunction in renal tubular epithelium produces nephronophthisis that frequently progresses to end-stage kidney disease, the major cause of morbidity and mortality. Hepatic fibrosis and retinal dystrophy reflect the same ciliopathy mechanism in hepatobiliary tissue and photoreceptors.

evidence.snippet: Most affected children develop nephronophthisis that often leads to end-stage kidney disease in infancy or childhood, a major cause of morbidity and mortality. Hepatic fibrosis and retinal dystrophy are also observed.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous IFT140 variants in two Polish families with Sensenbrenner syndrome and early onset end-stage renal disease.
evidence.snippet: Both patients had severe renal failure requiring kidney transplantation in early childhood.

nodes.description: Retinal dystrophy/degeneration as part of the ciliopathy phenotype of CED.

evidence.snippet: Hepatic fibrosis and retinal dystrophy are also observed.

nodes.description: Shortened proximal limbs (rhizomelic limb shortening) are part of the skeletal involvement in CED.

evidence.snippet: shortened proximal limbs, syndactyly

nodes.description: Premature fusion of the sagittal suture producing dolichocephaly, the craniofacial feature that distinguishes CED from most other ciliopathies.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies WDR35 variants involved in Sensenbrenner syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Sensenbrenner syndrome/cranioectodermal dysplasia (CED) is an autosomal-recessive disease that is characterized by craniosynostosis and ectodermal and skeletal abnormalities.

nodes.description: Growth deficiency / short stature is a core clinical feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: growth deficiency, and characteristic facial features

nodes.description: Sparse, slow-growing hair, a cardinal ectodermal feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: ectodermal features (widely spaced hypoplastic teeth, hypodontia, sparse hair, skin laxity, abnormal nails)

nodes.description: Increased distance between the inner canthi, part of the CED facial gestalt.

evidence.snippet: characteristic facial features (frontal bossing, low-set simple ears, high forehead, telecanthus, epicanthal folds, full cheeks, everted lower lip)

nodes.description: Widely spaced, hypoplastic teeth, an ectodermal feature of CED.

evidence.snippet: ectodermal features (widely spaced hypoplastic teeth, hypodontia, sparse hair, skin laxity, abnormal nails)
